content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Client Workflow to Solve Collaboration
content.seo_description: Learn how to optimize your client workflow and overcome collaboration silos. A high-impact guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Collaboration bottlenecks kill freelance velocity. The solution is an automated, transparent client feedback loop integrated directly into your dev environment. By syncing Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for real-time iterative changes, you transform client feedback into executable code, eliminating manual back-and-forth and ensuring production-ready shipping cycles.
content.intro:
You know the sound—the ping of a Slack message at 9 PM from a client asking for 'just one more change' that breaks your entire architecture. The silent friction of email threads, fragmented feedback, and version control hell isn't just annoying; it’s a direct tax on your profitability and sanity.

It is time to stop being a bottleneck and start acting as a system architect. By embedding your client directly into a high-velocity feedback loop, you move from 'service provider' to 'indispensable partner.' This guide delivers the technical blueprint to automate that workflow using the raw power of Antigravity and Claude Code.

steps.title: Establish the Source of Truth
steps.description: Create a unified project manifest that serves as the single point of contact for both you and the client, reducing ambiguity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a project manifest structure that defines scope, technical requirements, and a feedback loop protocol for an AI-integrated SaaS application.

steps.title: Automate Feedback Ingestion
steps.description: Configure an automated pipeline to parse client feedback notes into actionable technical tickets.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a feedback-to-task converter that maps unstructured client requests into specific codebase files for modification.

steps.title: Build the Architectural Scaffold
steps.description: Use Antigravity to build the core modular architecture that allows for isolated, safe feature updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a modular architecture for an AI-enabled dashboard that separates core logic from client-specific configuration files.

steps.title: Execute Iterative Updates
steps.description: Deploy Claude Code to apply requested changes precisely, ensuring no regression in core functionality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply the latest UI/UX feedback to the dashboard component while maintaining existing API integrations and type safety.

steps.title: Automate Client Sign-off
steps.description: Generate a summary of changes and a preview deployment link for instant client verification.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script that summarizes the changes made to the codebase and generates a markdown report for client review.

pro_tips: Never send a 'work in progress' email; only send links to live previews generated directly from your Claude Code pipeline.
Use Antigravity to define strict schema boundaries; it prevents clients from requesting features that break your core data model.
Tag every client request in your commit history to maintain an audit trail that justifies your billable hours.
Use local AI memory in Claude Code to store client preference patterns, effectively turning your assistant into a domain expert on their brand.
Always build a 'rollback' branch before applying client-requested changes to ensure you can revert in seconds if the architecture hits a wall.
content.next_steps: Begin by auditing your current project documentation; move your client's requirements into the Antigravity manifest format to establish your new, streamlined workflow.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical framework to eliminate collaboration friction and reclaim your billable hours. Stop chasing feedback and start engineering it. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
